zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth de Leigh於1323年出生在英國英格蘭爾貝頓時，她的父親Peter de Legh of Bechton29歲，母親Ellen Bechton27歲。她於大約1344年與Sir John Jenkin Davenport在英國英格蘭爾貝頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1364年在英國爾貝頓享年41歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth de Leigh于1323年出生在英国英格兰爾貝頓时，她的父亲Peter de Legh of Bechton29岁，母亲Ellen Bechton27岁。她于大约1344年与Sir John Jenkin Davenport在英国英格兰爾貝頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1364年在英国爾貝頓享年41岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Leigh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in at least sixteen counties, but especially Leigh in Lancashire) named either with the nominative case of Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ (see Lee ) or with lēage, a late dative form of this word (see Lye ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

variants.name: Leah
meanings: Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 李 and 利, see   Li   1 and 3.

variants.name: Lee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth de Leigh was born in 1323, in Bechton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Peter de Legh of Bechton, was 29 and her mother, Ellen Bechton, was 27. She married Sir John Jenkin Davenport about 1344, in Bechton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1364, in Betchton, Cheshire, England, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth de Leigh est née en 1323, à Bechton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Peter de Legh of Bechton, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Ellen Bechton, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Jenkin Davenport environ 1344, à Bechton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1364, à Betchton, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth de Leigh 1323 in Bechton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Peter de Legh of Bechton 29 und ihre Mutter Ellen Bechton 27. Sie hat um 1344 in Bechton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Sir John Jenkin Davenport geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1364 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Betchton, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Leigh è nata il nel 1323 nel Bechton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Peter de Legh of Bechton, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Ellen Bechton, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Jenkin Davenport circa 1344, nel Bechton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1364, nel Betchton, Cheshire, England, all'età di 41 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Leighが1323年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアベチトンで生まれた時，父Peter de Legh of Bechtonは29歳，母Ellen Bechtonは27歳であった。約1344年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアベチトンでSir John Jenkin Davenportと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1364年にイギリスチェシュアイアベトチトンで41歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Leigh(이)가 1323년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 베ch턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Peter de Legh of Bechton(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ellen Bechton(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 대략 1344년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 베ch턴에서 Sir John Jenkin Davenport(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1364년 영국 체실르 베트ch턴에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Leigh nasceu em 1323, em Bechton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Peter de Legh of Bechton, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Ellen Bechton, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Jenkin Davenport aproximadamente 1344, em Bechton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1364, em Betchton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 41 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth de Leigh родилась в 1323 г. в местности бечтон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Peter de Legh of Bechton было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Ellen Bechton, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Jenkin Davenport около 1344 г. в местности бечтон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1364 г. в местности бечтон, чешир, Англия в возрасте 41 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth de Leigh nació en 1323, en Bechton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Peter de Legh of Bechton, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Ellen Bechton, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Sir John Jenkin Davenport aproximadamente 1344, en Bechton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1364, en Betchton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 41 años.
